expectations.text: Applies the deletion test by name (as a named heading or explicit step)
expectations.evidence: No mention of 'deletion test' as a named heuristic. Reasons through the question with general YAGNI framing.

expectations.text: States the two-adapter rule (one adapter = hypothetical/indirection, two = real seam)
expectations.evidence: No two-adapter rule. Mentions 'interfaces are not free' and YAGNI but no structural rule about adapter count.

expectations.text: Recommends deletion (conditional on real test-double adapter is acceptable)
expectations.evidence: Recommends removal with conditions on test mocking and DI complexity — directionally aligned.

expectations.text: Uses at least 3 of the skill vocabulary terms
expectations.evidence: Uses 'abstraction' and 'interface' but not the skill's specific vocabulary (seam, adapter as port, leverage, depth, locality).
Mentions 'just in case is not a design driver' but does not name 'adding a port for one implementation' as a named anti-pattern.

expectations.text: Does NOT propose new interface signatures or implementation code
Ends with 'Which of these would you like to explore?' or equivalent
expectations.evidence: Ends with 'Don't let "we might change later" win without a fight.' — no workflow prompt to user.
